text_content: The sudden primal urge to get a cheetah after watching this is driving me crazy

As was also found out by the breeding experiment with foxes, the lower stress environment lowers the cortisol stress hormone which causes other neurochemical changes that increase docility and sociability. As with most animals, remove them from the stressful life of every day being a fight for survival for a few generations and you get a creature that has become so human adapted that their personalities can take a massive u-turn from the usual standoffishness and wariness of their wild counterparts. Adding _the_ dog breed that by default sees everything as a friend has fast tracked this in cheetahs.
Remember it is weakest in strength in big cats and less aggressive too but it can still f*ck you up if you trouble it....
